nodes.name: iterate_pdb_atoms
nodes.description: Iterates over all atoms in the PDB structure, optionally filtering by a specific chemical element.

inputs.name: element
inputs.constraints: If provided, only atoms of this element are yielded.

outputs.name: atom_iterator
outputs.constraints: An iterator yielding atom objects from the PDB structure.

nodes.conceptual_summary: filtered_collection_iterator. Sequentially traverses a structured collection of records, yielding a stateful iterator over the entire set. An optional filtering parameter allows for the selective retrieval of records where a specific categorical attribute matches a given value. This provides a memory-efficient mechanism for processing either the entire dataset or a specific subset without loading the full collection into memory.. Applications: In computer graphics, iterating through all nodes in a scene graph, with an option to filter for nodes of a certain type, like 'light', 'mesh', or 'camera'., In social network analysis, streaming through a dataset of user profiles, selectively yielding only users who match a specific demographic attribute (e.g., city or listed interest)., In financial data processing, scanning a large ledger of transactions and yielding only those associated with a particular transaction type or currency., In astronomical surveys, iterating through a catalog of celestial objects and filtering to return only objects of a specific class, such as 'galaxy' or 'quasar'.

nodes.name: iterate_pdb_residues
nodes.description: Iterates over all residues in the PDB structure. An optional filter can be applied to return only residues that contain a specified chemical element.

inputs.name: element
inputs.constraints: If provided, only residues containing an atom of this element are yielded.

outputs.name: residue_iterator
outputs.constraints: An iterator yielding residue objects from the PDB structure.

nodes.conceptual_summary: query_substructures_by_component_property. Traverses a hierarchical data structure composed of nested substructures, which in turn contain primitive components. It generates a sequential, lazy iterator over the substructures, with an optional filter to include only those that contain a primitive component matching a specific categorical property.. Applications: In a 3D scene graph, iterating over all mesh groups that contain a specific primitive type, like a light source or a collision object., In a file system representation, finding all directories that contain a file with a specific extension (e.g., '.log')., In a parsed XML/HTML document, yielding all parent nodes (e.g., '<div>' tags) that contain a child node with a specific attribute., In a logistics database, iterating through all shipping containers that are registered to be carrying a specific class of item (e.g., 'refrigerated goods').
